tools.name: check_domain
tools.description: Run the full email and DNS deliverability check for a domain: MX, SPF, DKIM, DMARC, DNS health, provider detection, and blacklists. Returns a letter grade (A to F), a 0 to 100 score, and every individual check with its RFC reference. Set include_smtp to also run a live SMTP probe of the mail servers

tools.name: check_mx
tools.description: Check the MX records of a domain: presence, hostnames, resolution, null-MX, and common misconfigurations.

tools.name: check_spf
tools.description: Check the domain SPF policy: presence, syntax, the all qualifier, and the 10-lookup limit.

tools.name: check_dmarc
tools.description: Check the domain DMARC policy at _dmarc: presence, policy strength (none/quarantine/reject), and reporting.

tools.name: check_blacklist
tools.description: Check whether the domain and its mail server IPs are listed on the Spamhaus (ZEN, DBL, ZRD, AuthBL), SpamCop, GBUdb and PSBL blocklists.

tools.name: check_dkim
tools.description: Check DKIM signing keys for a domain. Without a selector, common provider selectors are probed. Pass an explicit selector to look up that exact key at selector._domainkey.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: check_domain · Private data: check_mx · Public sink: check_domain · Destructive: check_domain.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: check_domain · Private data: check_mx · Public sink: check_domain · Destructive: check_domain

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
